title: Implement debounce
description:
## Implement `debounce`

Write a function `debounce(fn, wait)` that returns a debounced version of `fn`. Calling the debounced function repeatedly should only invoke `fn` once, `wait` ms after the last call.

**Signature**
```js
function debounce(fn, wait) { /* ... */ }
```

Your debounced function should forward arguments to `fn`.

title: Implement Array.prototype.myMap
description:
## Implement `Array.prototype.myMap`

Add a `myMap` method to `Array.prototype` that behaves like `Array.prototype.map`: it returns a new array with the callback applied to each element. The callback receives `(value, index, array)`.

Do not use the built-in `.map`.

title: Implement Array.prototype.myFilter
description:
## Implement `Array.prototype.myFilter`

Add a `myFilter` method to `Array.prototype` that behaves exactly like the built-in `Array.prototype.filter`. It should return a new array containing all elements that pass the test implemented by the provided callback.

Do not use the built-in `.filter`.

title: Implement Array.prototype.myReduce
description:
## Implement `Array.prototype.myReduce`

Add a `myReduce` method to `Array.prototype` that behaves like `Array.prototype.reduce`. It takes a callback `(accumulator, currentValue, index, array)` and an optional `initialValue`.

Do not use the built-in `.reduce`.

title: Implement once
description:
## Implement `once`

Write a function `once(fn)` that takes a function and returns a new function. The returned function can only be invoked once. Subsequent calls should return the value from the first call.

title: Implement sleep
description:
## Implement `sleep`

Write an async function `sleep(ms)` that pauses execution for `ms` milliseconds. It should return a Promise that resolves after the given delay.

title: Implement deepClone
description:
## Implement `deepClone`

Write a function `deepClone(obj)` that creates a deep copy of a given JSON-serializable object. Mutating the clone should not affect the original object.

title: Implement memoize
description:
## Implement `memoize`

Write a function `memoize(fn)` that returns a memoized version of `fn`. If the function is called with the same arguments, it should return the cached result instead of re-computing.

title: Implement Array Chunk
description:
## Implement Array Chunk

Write a function `chunk(array, size)` that splits an array into groups the length of `size`. If the array can't be split evenly, the final chunk will be the remaining elements.

title: Flatten Array
description:
## Flatten Array

Write a function `flatten(array)` that takes an array of arbitrarily nested arrays and returns a single flat array.

title: Implement Function.prototype.myBind
description:
## Implement `Function.prototype.myBind`

Add a `myBind` method to `Function.prototype` that acts like the native `bind`. It should return a new function that, when called, has its `this` keyword set to the provided value, with a given sequence of arguments preceding any provided when the new function is called.

title: Implement Promise.all
description:
## Implement `promiseAll`

Write a function `promiseAll(promises)` that mimics `Promise.all`. It takes an array of Promises and returns a single Promise that resolves to an array of the results. If any promise rejects, the returned promise must reject immediately with that error.

title: Implement curry
description:
## Implement `curry`

Write a function `curry(fn)` that curries the given function. Currying transforms `fn(a, b, c)` into callable forms like `fn(a)(b)(c)` or `fn(a, b)(c)` until all arguments have been provided.

title: Implement groupBy
description:
## Implement `groupBy`

Write a function `groupBy(array, keyOrFn)` that groups an array of objects by a specific property name or a grouping function. It returns an object where keys are the grouped values and values are arrays of the original objects.

title: Implement Promise.race
description:
## Implement `promiseRace`

Write a function `promiseRace(promises)` that takes an array of Promises and returns a Promise that fulfills or rejects as soon as the first promise in the iterable fulfills or rejects.

title: Valid Palindrome
description:
## Valid Palindrome

Write a function `isPalindrome(str)` that checks if a given string is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backwards). Ignore case and non-alphanumeric characters.

title: Object Map
description:
## Object Map

Write a function `objectMap(obj, fn)` that takes an object and a callback function. It should return a new object with the same keys, but the values are the result of passing the original value to the callback.

title: Unique Array
description:
## Unique Array

Write a function `unique(array)` that takes an array of primitive values and returns a new array with all duplicates removed. Do not use `Array.from(new Set(array))` if you want a challenge!

title: Delay Execution
description:
## Delay Execution

Write a function `delay(fn, wait)` that executes a given function `fn` after `wait` milliseconds. Pass any additional arguments provided to `delay` into the `fn`.

title: Count By
description:
## Count By

Write a function `countBy(array, iteratee)` that counts the occurrences of values returned from the iteratee function. Returns an object mapping the keys to the number of times they appeared.
